source_record.title: Identification of Therapy-Induced Clonal Evolution and Resistance Pathways in Minimal Residual Clones in Multiple Myeloma through Single-Cell Sequencing.
source_record.study_context: Clinical Cancer Research article using single-cell RNA sequencing on paired diagnostic and posttreatment bone marrow samples, including MGUS, newly diagnosed multiple myeloma, posttreatment multiple myeloma, and one healthy donor sample; public extraction uses PubMed metadata, PMC full text, and abstract-level study design/results.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source reports three posttreatment transcriptional evolution patterns: clonal elimination in MRD-negative patients, and clonal stabilization or clonal selection in MRD-positive patients.
limitations: Does not estimate how often each trajectory occurs across all multiple myeloma settings.
Does not interpret any individual MRD result.
Does not recommend treatment escalation, de-escalation, or sequencing.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source supports tracking transcriptional adaptation in residual malignant plasma cells as part of therapy-induced clonal evolution.
limitations: Transcriptional-state findings require source-method review before cross-study scoring.
Not evidence for a specific clinical action.
Not a replacement for validated MRD or response criteria.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source reports a metabolic shift toward fatty acid oxidation in cycling-resistant plasma cells.
limitations: Metabolism signal is hypothesis-generating for research extraction.
Does not establish a metabolic intervention strategy.
Does not prove clinical efficacy for targeting this state.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source reports that selective plasma cells favored NF-kB pathway activity.
limitations: Pathway preference is not a drug-target selection rule.
Does not rank NF-kB against other resistance mechanisms.
Requires expert review before claim upgrade.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source reports a close link between genetic and nongenetic mechanisms behind clonal evolution in multiple myeloma.
limitations: Correlation does not establish a clinical intervention point.
Does not support clone-only or state-only mechanism rankings.
Cross-study comparability requires consistent methods and expert review.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source reports variations in interactions between malignant plasma cells and the tumor microenvironment, with selective plasma cells showing strong interaction patterns.
limitations: Cell-interaction inference is not proof of patient-specific mechanism.
Does not establish a microenvironment-targeting treatment recommendation.
Needs source-specific method extraction before comparison with other papers.

what_this_supports: The geometry pilot can represent MRD-positive residual disease as a moving research structure rather than a single static state.
Public extraction templates should preserve MRD method, paired sample timing, single-cell assay context, clone-state fields, pathway-state fields, and microenvironment-interaction fields.
Genetic and nongenetic mechanisms should remain linked in the map until source-specific evidence justifies separation.
what_this_does_not_support: It does not provide treatment guidance, MRD interpretation, relapse prediction, clinical monitoring guidance, or trial recommendation.
It does not rank resistance mechanisms by frequency or importance across multiple myeloma.
It does not claim that targeting any extracted pathway cures or prevents relapse.
It does not replace expert review of MRD, response, or relapse evidence.

review_notes: Built as the first source-specific extraction companion to mrd-resistance-geometry-pilot-v0.
Source details checked against PubMed/PMC indexed metadata and publicly accessible abstract/full-text snippets.
All extracted signals are research-map signals and are not clinical advice.
